add_device.name: add_device
add_device.description: Add a Junos device to the in-memory inventory and persist to devices.json. Required fields: device_name, device_ip, username, auth (ssh_key or password). port defaults to 22. With clients that advertise elicitation, missing fields are prompted; otherwise the call returns MissingArguments.

AuthConfig.description:
Authentication config for a Junos device. Tagged enum mirrors the Python
repo's `auth.type` discriminator.

device_name.description: Device name/identifier in the inventory map.

commit_check_config.name: commit_check_config
commit_check_config.description: Validate a candidate configuration on a Junos router without committing (commit check). Loads config into a candidate, runs commit-check, returns {success, diff, error?}, then discards the candidate. Never activates config.

discard_candidate.name: discard_candidate
discard_candidate.description: Discard uncommitted candidate configuration changes on a Junos router (rollback 0), returning the candidate to the running config. Never changes the running config. Use to recover a candidate left dirty (e.g. 'configuration database modified').

execute_junos_command.name: execute_junos_command
execute_junos_command.description: Execute a Junos command on the router. Supports optional max_lines/max_bytes/tail output caps, and honors trailing '| last N' / '| count'.

command.description: The command to execute on the router.

max_lines.description: Cap output to at most N lines (head; use `tail` for the last N).

max_bytes.description: Hard byte cap on returned output.

tail.description: With `max_lines`, keep the LAST N lines instead of the first N.

execute_junos_command_batch.name: execute_junos_command_batch
execute_junos_command_batch.description: Run N operational CLI commands across M routers, parallel across routers, sequential per router. Returns a per-router array of {command, ok, value?, error?} entries. Supports optional max_lines/max_bytes/tail output caps, and honors trailing '| last N' / '| count'.

routers.description:
Routers to execute against. Must be non-empty. Accepts a list, or a
single router name; the keys `router` / `router_name` are also accepted.

commands.description: Operational CLI commands to run sequentially per router. Must be non-empty.

batch_timeout.description: Optional whole-batch wall-clock ceiling in seconds.

max_concurrent_routers.description: Maximum number of routers in flight concurrently.

max_lines.description: Cap output to at most N lines (head; use `tail` for the last N).

max_bytes.description: Hard byte cap on returned output.

tail.description: With `max_lines`, keep the LAST N lines instead of the first N.

execute_junos_pfe_command.name: execute_junos_pfe_command
execute_junos_pfe_command.description: Execute a single PFE-shell command on one router via 'request pfe execute target <fpc> command "<cmd>"'. Supports optional max_lines/max_bytes/tail output caps, and honors trailing '| last N' / '| count'.

pfe_command.description: PFE command to execute (no surrounding quotes).

max_lines.description: Cap output to at most N lines (head; use `tail` for the last N).

max_bytes.description: Hard byte cap on returned output.

tail.description: With `max_lines`, keep the LAST N lines instead of the first N.

fetch_file.name: fetch_file
fetch_file.description: Download a file from a Junos device's /var/tmp/<basename> to the host staging directory, with sha256 verification. Mirror of transfer_file.

router_name.description: Source router name (must exist in inventory and use ssh_key auth).

remote_path.description:
Basename of the file under the device's /var/tmp/. Must not contain
'/', '\\', or '..'. Same allowlist as transfer_file.

local_name.description:
Optional override for the local basename written under the staging
directory. Defaults to `remote_path`. Same allowlist applies.

force.description: Overwrite if local dest exists with different sha256. Default false.

verify.description: Post-fetch sha256 verification (local vs remote). Default true.

gather_device_facts.name: gather_device_facts
gather_device_facts.description: Gather Junos device facts from the router

get_junos_config.name: get_junos_config
get_junos_config.description: Get the configuration of the router

get_router_list.name: get_router_list
get_router_list.description: Get the Junos routers visible to this caller. Returns [] when the caller's router scope has no current inventory matches.

junos_config_diff.name: junos_config_diff
junos_config_diff.description: Get the configuration diff against a rollback version

list_staged_files.name: list_staged_files
list_staged_files.description: List host-staging files (always); also lists /var/tmp/ on a Junos device when router_name is supplied

router_name.description: Optional router name. If present, also lists the device's /var/tmp/.

load_and_commit_config.name: load_and_commit_config
load_and_commit_config.description: Load and commit configuration on a Junos router

commit_comment.description: Commit comment recorded in the device commit log.

confirm_timeout_mins.description:
If set, uses confirmed commit with auto-rollback after N minutes.
The router will automatically revert if not confirmed within this window.

reload_devices.name: reload_devices
reload_devices.description: Reload the inventory. With no args, re-reads the current --device-mapping. With file_name, swaps to a new inventory file. Reports added/removed/changed device names.

file_name.description:
Optional path to a different inventory file. If omitted, re-reads
the current --device-mapping.

render_and_apply_j2_template.name: render_and_apply_j2_template
render_and_apply_j2_template.description: Render a Jinja2 template (inline) with JSON vars. Optionally commit the rendered config to one or more routers; supports dry-run. (YAML vars are no longer accepted as of v0.5.2.)

template_content.description:
Jinja2 template content as a string (inline; no file path).
Capped at 64 KiB.

vars_content.description:
Vars as a JSON object string. Must deserialize to a top-level JSON
object. Capped at 64 KiB. YAML is **not** accepted as of v0.5.2
(RJMCP-SEC-002).

router_name.description: Single router to apply to. Mutually exclusive with `router_names`.

router_names.description: Multiple routers to apply to. Mutually exclusive with `router_name`.

apply_config.description: If false (default), only renders and returns the rendered string.

commit_comment.description: Commit comment recorded in the device commit log when applied.

dry_run.description: If true, runs lock + load + diff + rollback (no commit). Implies apply_config=true.

config_format.description: Override format detection ('set', 'text', 'xml'). Auto-detected if omitted.

transfer_file.name: transfer_file
transfer_file.description: Push a local file from the staging dir to /var/tmp/ on a Junos device via SCP. Idempotent on matching SHA-256.

router_name.description: Target router name (must exist in inventory and use ssh_key auth).

source_path.description: Basename of the file under the staging dir. Must not contain '/', '\\', or '..'.

force.description: Overwrite if dest exists with different sha256. Default false.

upgrade_junos.name: upgrade_junos
upgrade_junos.description: DESTRUCTIVE: installs a new Junos image and REBOOTS the device. Outage ~5-7 min. Requires confirm=true to proceed; first call with confirm=false returns a ConfirmationRequired error containing the upgrade plan (current version, target version, image, free disk, estimated outage). v1 supports standalone devices only; chassis clusters are refused.

router_name.description: Target router (must exist in inventory and use ssh_key auth).

source_path.description:
Basename of the staged image under the staging dir. Validated
against the same ASCII allowlist as transfer_file.

target_version.description:
Expected target version string, e.g. "25.4R1.12". Post-install
`show version` must match exactly or the call fails with
UpgradePostVerifyMismatch.

confirm.description:
REQUIRED to perform the destructive upgrade. Defaults to false.
When false the tool runs read-only pre-flight and returns the
upgrade plan as a ConfirmationRequired error.

timeout.description: Per-call outer timeout in seconds. Default 900 (15 min).

reboot_wait_secs.description:
Wall-clock budget for NETCONF to reopen after install + reboot.
Default 480 (8 min).
